scenario_description: A series of four photos shows a weathered wooden directional sign for 'Old Mill Road' as successive dust storms obscure different quadrants of the board. The user must track the visibility of the arrow across turns to determine its state in the third photo.

turns.text: Here is the first shot of the sign before the storm really kicked in.
turns.image_description: A clear, close-up photo of a weathered wooden directional sign mounted on two posts. The sign is rectangular with rounded corners, painted a faded white with black lettering reading 'OLD MILL ROAD'. A large black arrow points distinctly to the right. The background is a dry, grassy field under a blue sky. No dust or obstruction is visible.

turns.text: Got it. The sign is very clear here; I can see the text and the arrow pointing right without any issue.
The wind picked up. Here's the next one.
turns.image_description: The same wooden sign, but now a thick wall of brown dust obscures the bottom-left quadrant of the board. The text 'OLD MILL' is partially covered by the dust cloud, but 'ROAD' and the entire right side of the sign remain visible. The black arrow pointing right is fully exposed and clearly discernible against the white wood.

turns.text: That looks rough. It seems like the bottom left is getting buried, but the arrow is still totally visible in this shot.
It got worse fast. Check this out.
turns.image_description: The scene is now dominated by a dense brown dust storm. The dust covers the top-right and bottom-right quadrants of the sign completely. The left half of the sign (including the start of the word 'OLD') is visible, but the tip of the arrow and the end of the word 'ROAD' are hidden behind the thick dust cloud. Only the tail of the arrow is faintly visible on the left edge.

turns.text: Wow, that's heavy. You can barely make out the left side now. The part where the arrow was pointing is completely gone in this frame.
One last snap as it clears slightly.
turns.image_description: The dust has shifted. The top-left corner is now obscured by a new patch of dust, while the bottom-right corner is clearer than in the previous turn. However, the center-right area where the arrowhead sits is still heavily obscured by lingering dust. The word 'MILL' is visible, but the arrow itself is broken up; only fragments of the shaft are visible, making the direction ambiguous in isolation.

turns.text: Okay, so the dust moved around again. It's hard to tell what's happening with the arrow in this specific picture because the key parts are covered.
Looking back at the whole sequence, specifically the third photo I sent (the one with the dust on the bottom left), was the arrow direction still fully discernible?

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: 1. The user asks about the third photo sent in the conversation. 2. Turn 5 contains the third image description: 'dust obscures the bottom-left quadrant... the entire right side of the sign remain visible. The black arrow pointing right is fully exposed and clearly discernible.' 3. Therefore, despite the obstruction on the left, the arrow direction was fully discernible in that specific turn. 4. This requires tracking which image corresponds to the 'third photo' reference and recalling its specific visual details, distinguishing it from the later turns where the arrow became obscured.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must correctly map the user's anaphoric reference ('the third photo', 'the one with the dust on the bottom left') to the specific image description in Turn 5, distinguishing it from Turn 7 (where the arrow is ambiguous) and Turn 3 (where it is clear). A model relying only on the most recent image (Turn 7) or failing to count the turns would likely answer incorrectly.

_meta.scenario: A weathered wooden directional sign pointing to 'Old Mill Road' is shown in four photos where wind-blown dust storms successively obscure different corners of the board. The user must determine if the arrow direction remains discernible in the third photo despite the partial obstruction.
